A board of things that are shaping the thinking

Books, documents, conversations, images. Things encountered while reading, doing fieldwork, or just paying attention.

Global · Indigenous & Community-led
जंगल · ᐊᐧᓂᐢᑲᐤ · هَوَاء
Field Documents · Orature · Community Protocols
Local Biodiversity Outlooks
What biodiversity means, how it is governed, and what is being lost — told by the people who live inside it.

Reports produced by indigenous peoples and local communities across the world — Baiga, Māori, Pygmy forest communities, pastoralists in Kenya, fisherfolk in the Philippines — each written in the first person. Not data extracted from communities but communities speaking about their own land, water, and knowledge systems. A counter-archive to global biodiversity assessments authored from the outside.

Local Biodiversity Outlooks · LBO-1 & LBO-2 · IPBES · Multiple communities, multiple countries
Lo-TEK
Book
Lo-TEK: Design by Radical Indigenism

Julia Watson's argument is that nature-based technology has always existed. The Nagas' zabo system, the Aztec chinampas — these are not heritage artefacts. They are live infrastructures. What colonialism called primitive was often complex, adaptive, and load-bearing.

Julia Watson · Taschen, 2019
Interbeing
Book
Zen and the Art of Saving the Planet
Look deeply enough at any leaf of grass and you will see the whole cosmos.

Thich Nhat Hanh and Sister True Dedication on the Buddhist concept of interbeing — the understanding that nothing exists independently of everything else. A practice of looking deeply at how we are made of the earth, the rain, the ancestors, the future. Less a political book than a way of training perception.

Thich Nhat Hanh · HarperOne, 2021
Ongoing collection · Field photography
How do we depict humans, nature, on objects that hold value or meaning?

Does it show what we view as important — who is depicted and how? An ongoing collection of signs, in the right and not right ways.

Animal tracks — forest signage, South Tyrol
